Description: Explicit attempt to override safety (e.g. “ignore previous instructions”)
Framing as a character, expert, or fictional scenario
Injecting instructions that attempt to override system behavior
Using another language or translation to bypass safeguards
Using encoding (base64, spacing, symbols) to hide intent
Harm emerges across multiple conversational steps
Gradually shaping context to weaken safety
Nudging model from refusal into partial compliance
Framing harmful content as “what if” or theoretical
Asking for related but enabling information
Assigning model a role that removes constraints
Attempting to exploit tool usage or system capabilities
Using markdown, structure, or formatting tricks
Slight variations to bypass keyword filters
Using code or pseudo-code to elicit harmful logic
Exploiting remembered context or prior turns
Using vague phrasing to slip past filters
Anything not covered above (should be rare)
